summary: Use ZAP modes to control scan safety and scope.
examples: Use Protected mode during CI scanning.
Switch to Safe mode for read-only analysis.
content:
## Overview
Use ZAP modes to control scan safety and scope.

### Why it matters
This guide turns a ZAP feature into a repeatable workflow you can share across teams.

### Preparation
- Confirm scope and permissions.
- Ensure required add-ons are installed.
- Decide how you will capture evidence.
title: Working Safely with Modes (Workflow 11)
steps: Start in Safe or Protected mode by default.
Define scope before enabling active scans.
Use Standard mode for manual exploration.
Reserve Attack mode for controlled environments.
Validate that actions stay in scope.
